description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone wall forming the main body, set on a round, flat base that resembles compacted earth. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof made of overlapping, irregularly cut wooden shingles, supported by two sturdy wooden posts. A horizontal wooden axle runs between the posts, featuring a hand crank on one side and a central spindle, suggesting a mechanism for raising and lowering a bucket. The stonework is rough and uneven, giving the well a rustic, hand-built appearance. Scattered stones and pebbles are visible on the ground around the base, adding to the naturalistic setting. The overall construction appears robust and functional, suitable for outdoor urban or park environments.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden shingle roof, hand crank, and circular stone wall, set on a round earthy base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's stone wall, wooden posts, pitched shingle roof, and the hand crank mechanism clearly visible on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular profile of the roof, the thickness of the wooden post, and the circular stone well partially obscured.
description_view_2: Nearly identical to the front view, showing the well's stone wall, roof, and crank, but from a slightly different angle.
description_view_3: Rear side view focusing on the back of the pitched roof and the supporting post, with the stone well visible beneath.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A horizontal wooden axle with a hand crank is present, but no bucket or rope is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 0.7 meters high surrounds the well opening; wooden roof supported by two posts acts as a canopy.
